name: Business Cycles, 1831–1866 (Ayres Index)

book_quotes.quote: Figures 2.4A–C are monthly indicators of the cyclical component of business activity, compiled by the Cleveland Trust Company (Ayres 1939, table 9, appendix A, col. 1).
Figure 2.4A Business Cycles, 1831–1866. Figure 2.4B Business Cycles, 1867–1902. Figure 2.4C Business Cycles, 1903–1939. From Ayres (1939).

primary_source.publication: Ayres, L. P. (1939). Turning Points in Business Cycles. New York: Macmillan.
primary_source.units: Percent deviation from trend (cyclical component of business activity)
primary_source.replaced_by: No modern continuation; NBER macrohistory + BCDB business-cycle dates are the closest analogues.

concerns: Ayres (1939) is a pre-NBER composite indicator with no contemporary successor; NBER macrohistory monthly series (m12003 etc.) cover similar concept but are not the Ayres construction.

methodology_notes: Direct port of Ayres (1939) monthly cyclical-component series, 1831–1866 subperiod (Fig 2.4A).
CD2 dossier S004 notes ~12 obs/year and that pre-NBER composite indicators have no clean modern equivalent.
Same Ayres source feeds S205 (1867–1902, Fig 2.4B) and S206 (1903–1939, Fig 2.4C).
open_questions: Confirm whether anwarshaikhecon.org Appendix 2.2 still hosts the digitized Ayres data table (book references this URL on p. 763).
